channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: New Commissioner to strengthen voice for Aboriginal children and young people in Victoria
item.description: Victoria has appointed Dallas Widdicombe as the state’s new Commissioner for Aboriginal Children and Young People, marking an important step in strengthening advocacy, accountability and culturally grounded leadership for Aboriginal children and young people.
item.content:encoded: Victoria has appointed Dallas Widdicombe as the state’s new Commissioner for Aboriginal Children and Young People, marking an important step in strengthening advocacy, accountability and culturally grounded leadership for Aboriginal children and young people.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Safeguarding children in childcare: teacher confidence key to addressing trauma
item.description: From workforce shortages to child safety concerns, Australia’s early childhood education sector is under pressure. Despite these pressures, children should never bear the consequences, particularly those who have already experienced trauma.
item.content:encoded: From workforce shortages to child safety concerns, Australia’s early childhood education sector is under pressure. Despite these pressures, children should never bear the consequences, particularly those who have already experienced trauma.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Digital Child Annual Report 2025 highlights growing focus on children’s digital lives
item.description: The ARC Centre of Excellence for the Digital Child has released its 2025 Annual Report, offering a detailed snapshot of how Australian children are growing, learning and connecting in an increasingly digital world.
item.content:encoded: The ARC Centre of Excellence for the Digital Child has released its 2025 Annual Report, offering a detailed snapshot of how Australian children are growing, learning and connecting in an increasingly digital world.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Embedding road safety in early childhood settings: what Victorian services need to know
item.description: The Victorian Early Childhood Regulatory Authority (VECRA) has highlighted the importance of embedding road safety practices across early childhood education and care (ECEC) settings, reinforcing that road safety is not an “add-on” but part of everyday operations, family engagement and curriculum design.
item.content:encoded: The Victorian Early Childhood Regulatory Authority (VECRA) has highlighted the importance of embedding road safety practices across early childhood education and care (ECEC) settings, reinforcing that road safety is not an “add-on” but part of everyday operations, family engagement and curriculum design.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Sydney provider penalised after failing to comply with Fair Work Compliance Notice
item.description: A Sydney early learning provider has been penalised more than $11,000 after failing to comply with a lawful Compliance Notice issued by the Fair Work Ombudsman (FWO), in a case that reinforces the importance of wage integrity and timely regulatory response across the early childhood education and care (ECEC) sector.
item.content:encoded: A Sydney early learning provider has been penalised more than $11,000 after failing to comply with a lawful Compliance Notice issued by the Fair Work Ombudsman (FWO), in a case that reinforces the importance of wage integrity and timely regulatory response across the early childhood education and care (ECEC) sector.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: NSW and Victoria conduct nation-first early learning compliance blitz
item.description: The NSW Early Learning Commission and the Victorian Early Childhood Regulatory Authority (VECRA) have joined forces in a nation-leading compliance operation focused on child safety across the early childhood education and care sector.
item.content:encoded: The NSW Early Learning Commission and the Victorian Early Childhood Regulatory Authority (VECRA) have joined forces in a nation-leading compliance operation focused on child safety across the early childhood education and care sector.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 8 May 2026
item.description: This week’s SNAICC in the News remembers Kumanjayi Little Baby and reflects on Sorry Business, community healing and Catherine Liddle’s calls for care.

item.title: Investigation needed into child protection leaks
item.description: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children is calling for an immediate investigation into the leaking of confidential information relating to child protection notifications involving Kumanjayi Little Baby and her family
item.content:encoded: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children is calling for an immediate investigation into the leaking of confidential information relating to child protection notifications involving Kumanjayi Little Baby and her family
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 1 May 2026
item.description: This is not the news we wanted to hear. This week has been a tragedy that has shattered the nation and devastated the community of Mparntwe/Alice Springs.
item.content:encoded: This is not the news we wanted to hear. This week has been a tragedy that has shattered the nation and devastated the community of Mparntwe/Alice Springs.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: National Child and Family Investment Strategy sets clear direction, now governments must deliver

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 17 April 2026
item.description: Sector Transformation Principles Framework continued to draw national coverage, Australia’s under-16 social media restrictions turned to the risks for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander young people in regional and remote communities, and after the WA Government announced a $45 million child protection package, we reiterate our calls to implement the long-promised WA Roadmap, and more.
item.content:encoded: Sector Transformation Principles Framework continued to draw national coverage, Australia’s under-16 social media restrictions turned to the risks for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander young people in regional and remote communities, and after the WA Government announced a $45 million child protection package, we reiterate our calls to implement the long-promised WA Roadmap, and more.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC
